title: Aureline effective-scope resolution: requested action, target object, provider-declared scopes, local policy locks, trust posture, decision class, grant-resolution reason, least-privilege alternatives, and downstream invalidation vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the provider_scope_resolution_result_record, least_privilege_alternative_record, and effective_scope_invalidation_event_record frozen in docs/providers/connected_account_registry_contract.md. The resolution record combines provider-declared scopes, Aureline policy bundles (ADR-0008), object-level target rules, and local trust posture (ADR-0001) into one decision row reused by desktop, CLI, companion, audit, and support / export surfaces. Every record carries exactly one decision class (allowed, denied, browser_only, local_draft_only) and exactly one ADR-0010 grant_resolution_reason; permission explanations cite both so a surface can name the exact decision source and fallback path instead of one opaque 'Connected' or 'Not authorized' badge. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, raw policy-injector material, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary. The eventual connected-provider crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new requested_action_class, decision_class, alternative_class, policy_lock_class, or invalidation_trigger_class is additive-minor and requires an effective_scope_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

effective_scope_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this effective-scope vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / audit streams. Raw URLs, raw tokens, and raw provider payloads never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show on desktop, CLI, notification, provider-linked header, queue-review, and support-export surfaces.

provider_actor_class.description: Frozen provider-actor class vocabulary reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the resolution was computed under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0007 and ADR-0010 redaction posture.

mutation_mode.description: Mutation mode reused from ADR-0010. Disclosed at the point of intent.

decision_class.description: Top-level effective-scope decision class. Every resolution record names exactly one. allowed admits the requested action under the requested mutation mode; denied refuses with a typed grant_resolution_reason; browser_only routes through a typed browser_handoff_packet (ADR-0010); local_draft_only retains the action as a local_draft / deferred_publish queue item until prerequisites align. A single opaque 'Connected' or 'Not authorized' state is forbidden; the decision_class and grant_resolution_reason MUST always be visible together.

grant_resolution_reason.description: Frozen grant-resolution reason reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. Every resolution record names exactly one reason in addition to the top-level decision_class so callers can quote both the decision source and the fallback path.

requested_action_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for the requested action class the resolution adjudicates. Surfaces MUST quote this class on the resolution row; a surface that cannot place the requested action MUST route to read_only_inspection rather than default to a mutating class.

policy_lock_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for the local policy lock applied on top of provider-declared scope. Every resolution carries an array of locks (or no_local_policy_lock); a denied / browser_only / local_draft_only decision MUST cite at least one lock or one denial-class grant_resolution_reason so the user can repair.

alternative_class.description: Frozen vocabulary for the least-privilege alternative the resolution offers. A denied / browser_only / local_draft_only resolution MUST offer at least one alternative; no_alternative_available is repair-only and MUST be paired with an admin-routed repair hook.

invalidation_trigger_class.description: Frozen trigger vocabulary for an effective-scope invalidation event. Every event names exactly one trigger; cached scopes that depend on the affected actor class / host / tenant / policy epoch / trust state are invalidated deterministically and MUST visibly degrade rather than silently keep claiming authority.

downgrade_action_class.description: Frozen downgrade-action vocabulary reused from connected_account_record.schema.json so an effective-scope invalidation and an account invalidation render through the same vocabulary.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity of the object the resolution adjudicates.

policy_context.description: Policy context the resolution was computed under. Re-evaluated when a downstream invalidation fires.

freshness_block.description: Freshness posture for cached provider state the resolution depends on. bounded_stale MUST cite a freshness_floor_ref; unbounded_stale MUST carry a staleness_rationale.

narrowed_scope_refs.description: Opaque refs to the narrower provider-declared scopes the alternative would request.

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to the repair hook the surface routes the user to (account reselection, browser recovery, step-up authenticator, admin review). Empty string only when alternative_class is no_alternative_available.

allOf.description: no_alternative_available MUST cite a repair hook (admin reconciliation) or carry an explicit empty repair_hook_ref to make the absence visible.

least_privilege_alternative_record.description: One typed least-privilege alternative attached to a denied / browser_only / local_draft_only resolution. Surfaces render alternative_class plus alternative_summary; widening to a non-frozen alternative class is forbidden.

actor_subject_ref.description: Opaque ref to the human_account_link / installation_or_app_grant / delegated_credential_binding / project_scoped_grant / policy_injected_service_identity record the resolution acts under.

provider_declared_scope_refs.description: Opaque refs to the provider-declared scope rows currently held by the actor for the target. Raw provider scope tokens never appear here.

policy_lock_classes.description: Policy-lock array. A resolution with no local policy override MUST contain exactly one entry: no_local_policy_lock; a resolution with at least one active lock MUST list all of them.

decision_summary.description: Short reviewable sentence the desktop, CLI, notification, provider-linked header, and support-export surfaces render. Names the decision_class, the grant_resolution_reason, and (for non-allowed decisions) the next-safe-action.

approval_ticket_ref.description: Opaque ref to the approval-ticket record that admits the requested action. Required when decision_class is allowed and requested_mutation_mode is publish_now or open_in_provider; empty string for inspect_only and local_draft_only.

browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the browser_handoff_packet_record the user will be routed through. Required when decision_class is browser_only.

publish_later_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publish_later_queue_item_record the action is parked under. Required when decision_class is local_draft_only.

least_privilege_alternative_refs.description: Opaque refs to least_privilege_alternative_records offered alongside this resolution. Required (minItems 1) when decision_class is denied, browser_only, or local_draft_only.

expires_at.description: Optional expiry; an expired resolution is re-evaluated rather than reused. Resolutions whose underlying actor / scope / policy state changed before expiry are invalidated through an effective_scope_invalidation_event_record.

audit_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to the provider_handoff audit events emitted for this resolution (grant_scope_resolved, provider_action_proposed, provider_action_denied, provider_action_deferred).

allOf.description: decision_class allowed MUST pair with grant_resolution_reason in the allowed* family.
decision_class browser_only MUST pair with grant_resolution_reason allowed_with_browser_handoff and MUST cite a browser_handoff_packet_ref.
decision_class local_draft_only MUST pair with grant_resolution_reason allowed_with_deferred_publish and MUST cite a publish_later_queue_item_ref.
decision_class denied MUST pair with a denied_* grant_resolution_reason.
denied / browser_only / local_draft_only resolutions MUST offer at least one least_privilege_alternative_ref.
denied_workspace_trust MUST pair with a workspace_trust_restricted_lock policy lock so the surface can route to a trust-grant repair.
policy_bundle_forbids_action lock MUST be paired with a denied decision class.
actor_class unknown_actor_class MUST decide denied with grant_resolution_reason denied_unknown_actor_class.

provider_scope_resolution_result_record.description: One effective-scope decision row. Combines provider-declared scopes, local policy locks, trust posture, and the requested action / mutation mode into a single inspectable adjudication. Reused by desktop, CLI, companion, audit, and support / export surfaces; permission explanations cite decision_class plus grant_resolution_reason so the surface can name the exact decision source and fallback path.

originating_account_invalidation_event_r.description: Opaque ref to the upstream account_invalidation_event_record (in connected_account_record.schema.json) when the trigger originates in the registry. Empty string when the trigger originates in the policy resolver / freshness / approval-ticket lane.

invalidated_dependent_refs.description: Opaque refs to dependent resolution / approval-ticket / browser-handoff-packet / publish-later queue-item records invalidated by this event.

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to the repair hook the surface routes to (account reselection, browser recovery, step-up authenticator, admin reconciliation). Empty string only when downgrade_action_class is no_downgrade_required.

allOf.description: host_mismatch_detected, tenant_switch_detected, and org_membership_lost MUST NOT downgrade to no_downgrade_required.
Every non-no-downgrade event MUST cite a repair_hook_ref.

effective_scope_invalidation_event_recor.description: Downstream invalidation of cached effective-scope resolutions. Triggered by a connected-account invalidation, a policy-epoch roll, a trust-state downgrade, a freshness drift, an approval-ticket revocation, or a provider health degrade. Names the typed downgrade action surfaces MUST render; silent reuse of an invalidated resolution is forbidden.
